title: Pod Priority and PriorityClass
description:
## Pod Priority and PriorityClass

**PriorityClasses** assign a numeric priority to pods. When cluster resources are scarce, the scheduler preempts lower-priority pods to make room for higher-priority ones. This is a CKA exam topic.

**Your task:**

1. Create a **PriorityClass** named `high-priority` with:
   - `value: 1000000`
   - `globalDefault: false`
   - `description: "High priority workloads"`
2. Create a Pod named `critical-pod` using `nginx:alpine` that references the `high-priority` PriorityClass via `spec.priorityClassName`

```bash
# Verify:
kubectl get priorityclass high-priority
kubectl get pod critical-pod -o jsonpath='{.spec.priorityClassName}'
```

hints.title: Create the PriorityClass
hints.body: PriorityClass is a cluster-scoped resource (not namespaced). Higher value = higher priority.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: scheduling.k8s.io/v1
kind: PriorityClass
metadata:
  name: high-priority
value: 1000000
globalDefault: false
description: "High priority workloads"
EOF

hints.title: Reference PriorityClass in a Pod
hints.body: Set `spec.priorityClassName` in the pod spec to the name of your PriorityClass.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: critical-pod
spec:
  priorityClassName: high-priority
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:alpine
EOF

commands.command: kubectl get priorityclass high-priority -o jsonpath='{.globalDefault}' | grep -v '^$' || echo false
